items.q: What is Immobilien Bot?
items.a: Immobilien Bot is an app for finding a flat in Germany (iOS & Android). It scans {portalCount} real-estate portals around the clock and sends you a push notification the moment a new listing matches your search — often minutes before anyone else even sees it.

items.q: How does Immobilien Bot work?
items.a: You set once where and how you want to live: search area on the map, rooms, size, price and further criteria. After that the bot takes over — it checks the portals non-stop for new listings and alerts you in real time the moment a match goes online. A tap on the notification takes you straight to the original listing.

items.q: Is Immobilien Bot trustworthy?
items.a: Yes. Immobilien Bot only searches publicly available listings and links you directly to the original listing on the respective portal. There you apply yourself as usual — the bot gives you the head start, nothing more and nothing less. Over {downloads} downloads and {appStoreRating} stars on the App Store speak for themselves.

items.q: Does the bot apply automatically for me?
items.a: No — and that is on purpose. You decide yourself which listings to apply for. Immobilien Bot makes sure you see them among the first and can react in seconds with your saved application letter. There are no automated bulk applications.

items.q: Why does speed matter so much when searching for a flat?
items.a: According to Berliner Zeitung, a single housing project in Berlin drew around 43,000 applications in just 30 minutes — for 288 flats. Anyone who only sees a listing in the evening is often already too late. Immobilien Bot opens exactly that window for you with real-time notifications.

items.q: Which real-estate portals are searched?
items.a: Currently {portalCount} portals: the big nationwide ones like ImmoScout24, Immowelt, Kleinanzeigen and WG-Gesucht, plus regional providers and housing associations like Howoge, Gewobag or GAG Köln. In the app you switch portals on or off individually. You will find the full list in the portals section above.

items.q: Does Immobilien Bot work in my city too?
items.a: Yes. The nationwide portals cover all of Germany — from big cities to the countryside. On top of that come regional sources, currently focused on Berlin, Cologne and Hesse. You define your search area freely on the map.

items.q: How quickly am I notified — even when the app is closed?
items.a: In real time: as soon as a matching listing goes online, you get a push notification within seconds. The app does not need to be open for that — the bot keeps working independently of your device, even at night.

items.q: Can I set up several search areas?
items.a: Yes, up to three at once. You draw each area freely on the map or pick a radius — for example your preferred neighbourhood, the one next to it and the area around your work. Combined with rooms, size and price this creates a precise search profile.

items.q: Can I search for furnished flats, shared rooms or with a WBS?
items.a: Yes. Besides rooms, size and price you filter by advanced criteria such as furnished, shared room (WG), WBS, flat swap or interim and sublet. Listings that are only visible with “Plus” on ImmoScout24 can be excluded on request.

items.q: How does the Telegram connection work?
items.a: In addition to the push notification you can connect Immobilien Bot with Telegram — new listings then also land directly in your chat.

items.q: How does the bot help me with the application?
items.a: You save your application letter once in the app. In the listing overview you later copy it to the clipboard with a single tap — paste, send, done. So often only seconds pass between the push and your application.

items.q: What does Immobilien Bot cost?
items.a: You start with {trialDays} days free and without obligation. After that Immobilien Bot costs {price7d} for 7 days or {price1m} for a month — depending on how intensively you are searching right now.

items.q: How does the free trial work?
items.a: For {trialDays} days you use the full range of features: all portals, real-time push, all features. If the bot is not for you, you simply cancel within the trial period — then you pay nothing.

items.q: Can I cancel anytime?
items.a: Yes. The plan runs via your Apple App Store or Google Play and can be ended there anytime with a few clicks — no notice period, no phone call, no risk.

items.q: Are there hidden costs or premium tiers?
items.a: No. Every plan includes all features — real-time push, Telegram connection, favourites, application letter and all portals. There are no extra costs and no stripped-down basic version.

items.q: Which devices is the app available for?
items.a: Immobilien Bot is available for iOS on the Apple App Store and for Android on Google Play — with an identical range of features on both platforms.

items.q: Which languages does the app support?
items.a: German and English. You switch the language right inside the app — handy for international flat hunters and expats.

items.q: Is there a dark mode?
items.a: Yes. You switch between light and dark mode anytime.

items.q: What happens to my data?
items.a: Immobilien Bot only needs what is necessary for your search: your search profile and a sign-in via email, Apple or Google. All details are governed by the [[privacy policy]].

meta.title: About – Immobilien Bot
meta.description: Why Immobilien Bot exists: built in Berlin so you can search every portal at once and be the first to hear about the flats that fit you.

about.headline: Why Immobilien Bot exists.

intro.p1: The perfect flat. You tap “Enquire” — “This listing is no longer available.” Too late again.
intro.p2: And that’s the good case. At least you saw this one. Many you never see: they go online at midday while you’re at work — and they’re gone before you next look. The market happens. Without you.

unfair.p1: The housing market was never fair. Those with the right contacts get the flat — often before it even goes online. But it’s not only about who you know. It’s about where and when you search. And both work against you.

reasons.tempoBody: Hundreds of people apply to the most sought-after rental flats every day on average — 373 in Berlin, 277 in Hamburg, 237 in Munich.¹ And ImmoScout24 automatically takes a free listing offline as soon as 10 enquiries come in.² So the good, affordable flat is often gone within minutes — vanished before you even saw it. No one who works and sleeps can sit there and refresh at exactly the right moment. And part of your competition has long been searching automatically.
reasons.reachBody: You search on two or three portals. But in the big cities the asking rent is often far above what searchers can pay — around 89% higher in Berlin, 56% in Hamburg.¹ The affordable flats do exist — they’re just elsewhere: with the municipal housing associations, from Degewo in Berlin to GAG in Cologne. On portals most people don’t even know exist.
reasons.close: Being fast is useless on the wrong sites. Knowing every portal is useless if you’re too slow. You’d have to be both: everywhere — and first. No human can do that.

tiles.label: search for longer than a year
contact enquiries per day on average for Berlin’s most sought-after flats
enquiries, then a free listing goes offline automatically

quote.t: The flat doesn’t go to whoever earns the most. It goes to whoever sees it

origin.p1: At the end of 2021, Juri and his family started looking for a flat in Berlin. The reason: a baby on the way. It quickly became clear: searching manually takes far too much time and barely fits into everyday life. And at some point the realisation: the flats are there. You just hear about them too late — and many sit on portals you never open.
origin.p2: So Juri built a tool that searched around the clock for him. It started as a simple Telegram notification for Kleinanzeigen. But soon friends and acquaintances asked for something similar.
origin.p3pre: Today the Immobilien Bot app scans
origin.p3post: , around the clock — across all of Germany. The big nationwide ones — ImmoScout24, Immowelt, Kleinanzeigen, WG-Gesucht, Vonovia. And the ones hardly anyone checks — the municipal housing associations in Berlin, Cologne and Hesse.
origin.p5: The moment a flat appears that fits you, you get it straight away — by push, in the very minutes it’s even visible. Not as number 200. As one of the first.

believe.p1: Everyone deserves a home. Not just those with the right contacts. Not just those who refresh portals all day. Not just those who happen to know the right portal.
believe.p2: Immobilien Bot is your edge — fast enough to be first, and wide enough to search everywhere. Whether you work full-time, have kids or are new to the city.
believe.p3: You’ve waited long enough. Time for the flat to come to you.

about.ratingsLine: {rating}★ on the App Store · {downloads} downloads · as of {asOf}
footnotes: ImmoScout24 analysis “Germany’s most-searched rental flat”, Q1 2024, top 10% of listings per region: on average 373 contact enquiries per listing per day in Berlin, 277 in Hamburg, 237 in Munich; asking rent around 89% (Berlin) and 56% (Hamburg) above searchers’ rent budget.
ImmoScout24, free basic listing (private landlords): runs for 14 days, maximum 10 contact enquiries; the listing ends automatically.
ImmoScout24 survey of 1,183 people with an active search request (flat or house, rent or buy), March–April 2024: 54.4% search for longer than a year.

footer.signature: The original. Faster than the rest.
sources: Immobilien Bot user data. For comparison: 54% of home seekers search for more than a year (ImmoScout24, 2024).
